Six questions per paper.

records.t: AlexNet · decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Krizhevsky, Sutskever, Hinton 2012 — the paper that started the modern deep learning era. ImageNet classification with deep convolutional neural networks. Plain English.
h: The paper that started everything.

records.t: AlphaFold 2 · decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Jumper et al. 2021 — the DeepMind paper that solved a 50-year-old grand challenge in biology by predicting the 3D structure of any protein from its amino acid sequence. Plain English. The biggest non-LLM AI breakthrough.
h: A fifty-year-old problem, solved.

records.t: AlphaGo — How DeepMind Cracked Go and Ended a 2,500-Year-Old Frontier · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: DeepMind built a Go-playing program that combined deep neural networks trained on human games with reinforcement learning self-play and Monte Carlo tree search, and in March 2016 it beat 18-time world champion Lee Sedol 4-1 in Seoul — twenty years earlier than expert forecasters predicted.

records.t: AlphaZero — One Algorithm Learns Chess, Shogi, and Go From Scratch in Hours · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: A single learning algorithm, given only the rules of the game and no human games to study, played millions of games against itself and within hours became the strongest known player at chess, shogi, and Go — demonstrating that a machine can reach superhuman skill in complex domains with zero human strategic knowledge a

records.t: Attention Is All You Need · decoded · /research/decoded/attention-is-all-you-need · AtomEons
records.d: The 2017 paper by Vaswani et al. that introduced the transformer architecture. Plain English. Why it created every AI you
h: Attention Is All You Need.
records.b: AtomEons / Research / Decoded / Attention Is All You Need

records.t: BERT — the language model that reads both directions at once · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: BERT showed that a transformer trained to fill in missing words by looking at context on BOTH sides of the gap — instead of left-to-right like GPT — learns a much deeper understanding of language, and that single model can be fine-tuned to dominate a dozen unrelated language tasks.

records.t: Chain-of-Thought Prompting · decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Wei et al. 2022 — the Google paper that proved asking AI to
h: “Think step by step.” That was the unlock.

records.t: The 4% Rule, Decoded — What Trinity Actually Said About Retiring Without Going Broke · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: If you save a pile equal to 25× your annual spending and invest it 50–75% in stocks, history says you can pull 4% of that pile in year one, raise the dollar amount with inflation every year after, and have a 95%+ chance of not running out in 30 years — which means most middle-class earners can mathematically retire dec

records.t: Constitutional AI · decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Bai et al. 2022 — the Anthropic paper that introduced training AI to behave well using a second AI as the grader instead of thousands of human labelers. Plain English. The technique behind Claude.
h: AI grading its own homework.

records.t: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO): Skipping the Reward Model in Alignment · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: You can fine-tune a language model on human preferences (this answer is better than that one) using ordinary supervised training — no separate reward model, no reinforcement learning loop — by treating the language model itself as its own reward function.

records.t: Five Minutes a Day Buys You Three Extra Years (And Why the Gym Industry Will Never Say It Out Loud) · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Running as little as 5–10 minutes per day, at slow speeds under 6 mph, cuts your risk of dying from any cause by about 30% and adds roughly 3 years to your life — and running more than that adds almost nothing on top.

records.t: What Fasting Actually Does to Your Cells (Ohsumi
records.d: Skipping meals for 12–24 hours triggers a real, Nobel-Prize-validated cellular cleanup process called autophagy — but most of the dramatic numbers you

records.t: Generative Adversarial Networks · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Two neural networks compete — one tries to forge realistic data, the other tries to spot the forgery — and the forger gets so good that its fakes become indistinguishable from real samples.

records.t: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (GPT-3) · decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Brown et al. 2020 — the OpenAI paper introducing GPT-3 and the discovery that very large language models can learn new tasks from a handful of examples without retraining. Plain English. The moment AI started
h: The moment AI started talking back.

records.t: Latent Diffusion · decoded · AtomEons
records.d: Rombach et al. 2022 — the paper that made high-quality AI image generation possible on consumer hardware. Stable Diffusion, Flux, Nano Banana Pro all descend from this. Plain English.
h: AI art on a laptop GPU.

records.t: Mamba — Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces · Research / Decoded · AtomEons
records.d: A neural network architecture called Mamba processes long sequences of text in time that grows linearly with the length, rather than quadratically like transformers, while matching their accuracy on many benchmarks — making it the most credible structural challenger to the transformer in years.

records.d: A language model that, before it answers your question, first searches a separate library of documents and reads the relevant ones — instead of trying to remember everything from training.
